texto: There’s a pause so yawning I can’t help but think about what it would be like to lean in and kiss her, but if I’m getting the signals wrong then I’m about to destroy the best run we’ve had all evening. It’s been at least ten minutes since I’ve done or said anything stupid.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Leanne Hall: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a amor. O trecho “There’s a pause so yawning I can’t help but think about what it would be like to lean in and kiss…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, heart, kiss pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de kiss e romantic.

texto: They envy your youth. You two are like exotic creatures from the land of the young.’I’ve never understood all that these-are-the-best-years-of-your-life crap. If this is as good as it gets then I might as well quit now. Let me get to the shimmery oasis of adulthood.

You two are like exotic creatures from the land of the young.’I’ve never un…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, young, youth pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de youth.

frase_original: They envy your youth. You two are like exotic creatures from the land of the young.’I’ve never understood all that these-are-the-best-years-of-your-life crap. If this is as good as it gets then I might as well quit now. Let me get to the shimmery oasis of adulthood.
